exemplar.title: To what extent does Beston Groupâs plastic pyrolysis plant contribute to the circular economy of Indonesia and, thereby, impact Indonesiaâs environmental and economic stability? (Chemistry And Economics)

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Beston Groupâs plastic pyrolysis plant contribute to the circular economy of Indonesia and, thereby, impact Indonesiaâs environmental and economic stability? (Chemistry And Economics)

strengths: Research question is clearly stated and sharply focused, specifying technology, company, country and impact dimensions
Topic is communicated clearly and consistently throughout the document, with minimal drift
Interdisciplinary scope is well articulated, linking chemistry and economic analysis effectively
weaknesses: Methodology justification is uneven, particularly in the representativeness of the Labuan Bajo model
Occasional repetition of the research question and lengthy exposition creates minor clutter
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay consistently presents a clear, focused research question and communicates the topic effectively across the introduction and headings, meriting a highâband score. However, justification of source selection and the leap from caseâstudy to nationalâscale analysis remain underdeveloped, preventing the award of the maximum 6 marks.

strengths: Terminology and concepts are used accurately and with depth (e.g., Î²-scission, circular economy)
Source material is broadly relevant, with over 100 entries spanning peer-reviewed journals and authoritative reports
Knowledge is coherently integrated across chemical mechanisms and economic models, demonstrating interdisciplinary fluency
weaknesses: A noticeable proportion of sources are non-peer-reviewed or corporate webpages, which limits academic rigour
Some process descriptions rely on oversimplified educational sources rather than specialist literature
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates coherent, integrated knowledge and consistently accurate use of discipline-specific terminology, justifying placement in the top band. Minor reliance on less rigorous sources and occasional oversimplifications prevent the award of the full 6 marks.

strengths: Research is generally well selected and relevant, drawing on scientific journals, international reports and local studies
Analysis links quantitative chemical data to broader economic implications, supporting largely coherent conclusions
Argument structure is logical and interdisciplinary, with counter-arguments acknowledged and evaluated
weaknesses: Some extrapolations (e.g., scaling town-level data nationally) lack full evidential backing
Critical evaluation, while present, could be more incisive in interrogating assumptions and uncertainties
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits a strong, well-supported analysis and a coherent interdisciplinary argument, meeting most descriptors in the 7â9 band and touching on features of the 10â12 band. However, remaining gaps in depth of evaluation and evidence support warrant a score just below the maximum.

strengths: Clear academic structure with coherent headings, numbered figures and a logical flow
Consistent referencing and bibliography formatting, including word count and page numbers
Well-chosen, correctly labelled visuals that directly support the analysis
weaknesses: Minor layout issues (figures referenced before placement) create brief reader disconnects
Repetition of the research question and inclusion of decorative images introduce unnecessary clutter
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is coherent and meets academic conventions convincingly, with strong visuals and referencing practice. Only minor formatting and stylistic lapses prevent the award of the maximum 4 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The argument follows a logical sequenceâcontext â chemical feasibility â environmental impacts â economic analysis â policyâfacilitating reader understanding.  
â¢ Interdisciplinary links are explicitly sign-posted; counter-arguments (energy intensity, price volatility) are acknowledged, adding critical insight.  
â¢ Nonetheless, the balance between description and evaluation is uneven, and certain assertions (job creation numbers) are accepted at face value. Consistency and some criticality justify a score of 5 in the 4-6 band.

The student uses footnotes and a 50-page bibliography in (largely) consistent MLA-style format; in-text superscripts link to footnotes, and a word count plus page numbers are provided.  
â¢ Occasional inconsistencies (broken URLs, mixed citation styles for some entries) and minor typographic errors are evident, but sources can be traced without difficulty.  
â¢ These small lapses place the work comfortably in the 3-4 band, with a score of 3 reflecting good but not flawless execution.

The student introduces an explicit, interdisciplinary topicâevaluating a specific companyâs plastic-pyrolysis plant in relation to Indonesiaâs circular economyâthat is foregrounded in the title, abstract and opening section.  
â¢ The focus on a single firm (Beston Group) and a defined national context (Indonesia) keeps the exploration appropriately narrow, while frequent restatement of the research question maintains clarity for the reader.  
â¢ Communication is largely precise and sustained, though occasional repetition and lengthy exposition create minor clutter. Overall, the topic remains clear, relevant and suitably delimited, meriting placement in the 5-6 band rather than the mid-band.

The essay follows a clear academic structure: title page, abstract, table of contents with logical headings, numbered figures/tables, and a conclusion.  
â¢ Sectioning aligns with the research question and aids coherence.  
â¢ Minor issuesâoccasional over-long headings and duplication of the research question on nearly every pageâslightly detract from polish, keeping the work at the lower end of the 3-4 band (score 3).

The student demonstrates solid disciplinary knowledge: detailed chemical mechanisms (thermal-oxidative degradation, zeolite-catalysed cracking) are explained alongside economic models (LRAS shift, costâbenefit).  
â¢ Sources are integrated in calculations of COâ emissions, fuel yields and fiscal projections, showing more than descriptive use.  
â¢ Yet synthesis sometimes lapses into lengthy description, and the link between the two disciplines is occasionally asserted rather than analysed. The work thus meets the descriptors for the 3-4 band at the upper level (score 4).

The essay applies quantitative calculations (COâ emissions, mass balances, pay-back period) and compares theoretical and real yields, leading to a nuancedââmoderate contributionâ-âconclusion.  
â¢ Analysis links chemistry findings (fuel quality) to economic implications (energy security), showing interdisciplinary thinking.  
â¢ Some leaps (extrapolating 2.5 % town fuel coverage to national impact) lack full evidential support, so conclusions are âlargelyâ rather than âfullyâ supported, placing the work at 5 within the 4-6 band.

The research questionââTo what extent does Beston Group Pte Ltdâs plastic pyrolysis plant contribute to the circular economy of Indonesia and thereby impact Indonesiaâs environmental and economic stability?ââis stated verbatim on the title page, in the introduction, and in headers.  
â¢ It is sharply focused, specifying technology, company, country, and two explicit dimensions (environmental and economic).  
â¢ While ambitious, the question is answerable within 4â000 words and guides the subsequent structure of chemistry- and economics-based analysis. Minor breadth issues (national-scale claims built on a single case study) keep the score at 5 rather than the maximum 6.

Most research selected is pertinent: scientific journals underpin chemical sections, World Bank and UNDP reports support economic parts, and local Indonesian studies ground the Labuan Bajo model.  
â¢ The majority of evidence is credible, but dependence on some promotional/company data and popular media reduces rigour.  
â¢ Overall selection aligns with the 4-6 band; breadth and relevance merit a mid-band score of 5.

The methodology section explains that the study relies on secondary literature, spectral data (FT-IR, GC-MS, NMR) and a published mass-balance model from Labuan Bajo to estimate yields and economic outcomes.  
â¢ Methods are broadly appropriate and the interdisciplinary link between chemistry (emissions/characterisation) and economics (costâbenefit, policy) is justified.  
â¢ However, justification is uneven: selection criteria for sources, limitations of using promotional company data, and reliability of extrapolating local figures to national scale are acknowledged only briefly. The design is competent but not yet âcomplete and effective,â positioning it solidly in the 3-4 band at the upper end (score 4).

The bibliography is extensive (>100 entries) and combines peer-reviewed journals, international reports and industry sources; most citations directly inform the chemical or economic analysis.  
â¢ Nevertheless, a noticeable proportion of popular or corporate webpages (Investopedia, Beston marketing material) reduces overall academic rigour, and primary data are absent.  
â¢ Relevance is mostly sound but not expert-level; hence the strand fits the 3-4 band, with a score of 4.

Numerous visuals (bar charts, flow diagrams, FT-IR spectra) are integrated and referenced beneath each figure.  
â¢ Captions are informative, and most images support discussion directly (e.g., Figure 8 informs fuel quality analysis).  
â¢ A few illustrations are repetitive or decorative (cartoon, sunset photo), and resolution/format vary, so presentation is competent rather than exemplary, warranting a score of 3.

Terminology such as âvolatile matter,â âÎ²-scission,â âcircular economy,â and âcompounded annual growth rateâ is used accurately and consistently.  
â¢ A glossary is appended, diagrams are captioned with appropriate chemical/economic vocabulary, and calculations follow accepted conventions.  
â¢ Minor slips (e.g., â$c6$-valerolactoneâ typesetting) do not impede understanding. Depth and correctness justify entry into the top band, though occasional verbosity restrains the mark to 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: Figure 1 is wellâlabelled and clearly sourced; it effectively supports the narrative of rising plastic waste generation and treatment trends.
Table 2 compares physical properties of pyrolysis oils comprehensively, highlighting differences in density, calorific value and flash point across feedstocks.
The analysis of oil yields is based on a single study; crossâverify key yield figures with additional literature to strengthen the validity of conclusions.
References to Figure 2 precede its placement, causing a temporary disconnect; reorder text so figures appear before or immediately after their first mention.
Citing a generic ScienceDirect homepage limits traceability of the specific article; replace with a direct DOI or precise article link to improve source relevance.
The section on plastic production accurately describes fractional distillation, using correct terminology and connecting crude oil processing to polymer feedstocks.
Relying on a GCSEâlevel BBC Bitesize source for distillation oversimplifies the process; cite a peerâreviewed chemical engineering reference to enhance academic rigor.
The title page repeats the research question verbatim twice, creating unnecessary clutter. Consider removing one instance to streamline reader focus and improve academic precision.
The decorative cartoon illustration is visually engaging but does not directly inform your analysis; consider replacing or supplementing with data-driven figures to strengthen the academic focus.
Including the word count at the start of the report meets the formatting requirement and demonstrates adherence to IB guidelines.
The table of contents is wellâstructured with clear sectional headings, aiding reader navigation and demonstrating strong academic organization.
The research question appears repeatedly on this page, which interrupts reading flow; consider referencing it sparingly after the introduction.
The introduction integrates robust global and Indonesian plasticâwaste statistics, demonstrating comprehensive knowledge and grounding the issue effectively.
The introduction provides context on plastic waste but lacks an explicit link to Beston Groupâs case study; include a sentence that ties this context directly to your research focus.
Footnotes use mixed citation formats and inconsistent date styles; adopt a uniform style to enhance referencing clarity and professionalism.
Extrapolating local landfill statistics to national conclusions lacks discussion of methodological limitations; address potential biases in scaling smallâarea data to whole Indonesia.
The methodology outlines data sources but provides minimal justification for selecting the Labuan Bajo model; expand on why this site is representative for national scale analysis.
Reliance on secondary data introduces potential bias; discuss triangulation or validation steps taken to ensure source reliability and mitigate promotional company data.
The interdisciplinary approach is articulated clearly, demonstrating integration of chemistry and economics to assess feasibility and broader impacts.
The final sentence of the methodology section is incomplete (âHowever, there is a challenge inâ¦â); ensure all paragraphs are fully developed and clear.
Table 1 showcases key polymer properties with accurate chemical and physical data, demonstrating strong disciplinary knowledge.
Limiting the analysis to PET, HDPE and PVC overlooks the prevalence of LDPE and PP in mixed waste; consider expanding to reflect actual waste composition.
The fractionating column diagram in Figure 3 is clear, wellâlabelled and complements the text explanation of separation processes.
Discussion of catalytic cracking would benefit from quantitative data (e.g., conversion rates or yields) to substantiate claims about zeolite performance.
The environmental implications of catalyst disposal are not addressed; include discussion of catalyst regeneration or waste handling to complete the analysis.
The mechanism description omits reaction kinetics and catalyst loading details; consider including rate data or operating conditions to strengthen the technical analysis.
Flowchart 1 effectively contrasts thermal and catalytic pyrolysis, demonstrating clear understanding of process variations and product characteristics.
The chemical reaction for PET methanolysis is presented with clear LaTeX notation, aiding precision and readability of the mechanism.
Figure 4b clearly visualizes PET hydrolysis, integrating physical and chemical steps to illustrate depolymerization pathways.
Impacts of acidâbased purification (e.g., equipment corrosion rates) are mentioned only qualitatively; provide quantitative data or mitigation strategies.
